datasets.name: session_event_log
datasets.source: Supabase event instrumentation — screen-level telemetry injected into existing FastAPI endpoints by Atlas/Pulse within Sprint 1
datasets.format: PostgreSQL table: {user_id, session_id, event_type, screen_name, timestamp, metadata_jsonb}
datasets.size_estimate: ~50K rows in first 30 days at current user volume; <5MB
datasets.cost: free — built on existing Supabase instance

datasets.name: ai_inference_cost_log
datasets.source: LLM provider API response metadata (token counts, model tier, latency) captured via FastAPI middleware and written to Supabase; backfilled from OpenAI/Anthropic usage dashboard for trailing 30 days
datasets.format: PostgreSQL table: {request_id, user_id, session_id, model_tier, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, cost_usd, timestamp}
datasets.cost: free — middleware instrumentation on existing stack

datasets.name: supabase_schema_audit
datasets.source: Supabase introspection query (pg_catalog) + manual review by Atlas — exported as annotated ERD and table inventory
datasets.format: Markdown + JSON: {table_name, columns, relationships, row_estimates, indexing, notes_on_memory_compatibility}

datasets.name: competitor_onboarding_teardown
datasets.source: Manual screen-capture walkthroughs of Fitbod, Future, and Freeletics onboarding flows — conducted by App Experience Studio within 48-hour bounded sprint; supplemented by existing teardown repositories (Mobbin, UserOnboard archive)
datasets.format: Structured JSON per app: {app_name, onboarding_steps[], time_to_first_workout_seconds, intake_field_count, perceived_value_moment, screenshots_path, notes}
datasets.cost: ~$30 — app subscription costs for 1-month access to Future ($19) and Freeletics ($11); Fitbod has free tier

datasets.name: competitor_app_store_review_corpus
datasets.source: App Store and Google Play reviews for Fitbod, Future, Freeletics, Caliber, Noom — scraped via app-store-scraper (Python OSS) or purchased via AppFollow/Sensor Tower API; minimum 500 reviews per app, filtered to trailing 12 months
datasets.format: JSONL: {app_name, review_id, rating, review_text, date, platform} + embedded vectors via text-embedding-3-small for semantic clustering
datasets.size_estimate: ~2,500 reviews raw (~3MB text); vector store ~15MB at 1536 dimensions
datasets.cost: ~$0 if OSS scraper used (app-store-scraper); ~$50/month if AppFollow tier required — recommend OSS first

datasets.name: behavioral_economics_evidence_library
datasets.source: PubMed / PsycINFO / Google Scholar — curated set of 40–60 peer-reviewed papers on commitment devices, implementation intentions, variable reward schedules, and loss aversion in mobile health and fitness behavior change contexts; augmented with Nir Eyal Hook Model documentation
datasets.format: PDF → chunked text → JSONL with metadata: {paper_id, title, authors, year, abstract, key_finding, mechanic_type, study_population, effect_size}; embedded for RAG retrieval
datasets.cost: ~$0 — PubMed open access + Semantic Scholar API (free tier); ~$20 if paywalled papers required via Unpaywall fallback

datasets.name: exercise_science_constraint_corpus
datasets.source: NSCA guidelines, ACSM Position Stands, peer-reviewed periodization literature — 30–50 documents covering progressive overload principles, minimum recovery intervals, movement pattern balance, beginner programming guardrails; supplemented by ExRx.net structured exercise taxonomy
datasets.format: Chunked JSONL with metadata: {source, document_type, constraint_type, rule_statement, population_scope, evidence_grade}; embedded for retrieval by Workout Program Studio and Algorithm Lab
datasets.cost: ~$0 — ACSM/NSCA guidelines are publicly available or accessible via open repositories; ExRx.net scrape is permissible for internal use

datasets.name: exercise_catalog
datasets.source: Curated from ExRx.net taxonomy + Wger open-source exercise database (MIT license) + any existing TransformFit internal exercise list — normalized by Atlas into Supabase
datasets.format: PostgreSQL table: {exercise_id, name, primary_muscle_group[], secondary_muscle_group[], equipment_required[], difficulty_tier, movement_pattern, contraindications, demo_url}
datasets.cost: free — wger database is open-source; ExRx taxonomy is manual curation

datasets.name: user_memory_store
datasets.source: Designed and built by Atlas — new Supabase table(s) for persistent user fitness profile, session history summary, coaching context snapshot, and goal state; populated by Nova's selective context injection layer
datasets.format: PostgreSQL JSONB key-value store: {user_id, profile_snapshot_jsonb, session_history_summary_jsonb, last_coaching_context_jsonb, goal_state_jsonb, updated_at}
datasets.cost: free — additive schema on existing Supabase instance

datasets.name: llm_pricing_and_routing_reference
datasets.source: OpenAI pricing page, Anthropic pricing page, and published prompt caching documentation — point-in-time snapshot captured by Ledger and Algorithm Lab; refreshed monthly
datasets.format: JSON: {provider, model_id, input_cost_per_1k_tokens, output_cost_per_1k_tokens, context_window, caching_available, caching_discount_rate, recommended_use_case}

datasets.name: user_cohort_retention_baseline
datasets.source: Reconstructed from existing Supabase auth logs and any available session records — computed by Pulse using signup timestamp vs. last_active_at to derive Day-1, Day-3, Day-7 retention curves for current user base
datasets.format: PostgreSQL materialized view: {cohort_week, day_1_rate, day_3_rate, day_7_rate, day_30_rate, n_users, computed_at}
datasets.size_estimate: <1MB; reference dataset for all Phase 3 pre/post comparison
datasets.cost: free — computed from existing auth data

datasets.name: user_intake_response_distribution
datasets.source: Extracted from existing Supabase user profile records by Pulse — frequency distribution of self-reported fitness goals, experience levels, equipment access, and schedule preferences from current user base
datasets.format: PostgreSQL aggregation table: {field_name, field_value, count, percentage, updated_at}; exported to JSON for Algorithm Lab cold-start prior construction
datasets.cost: free — computed from existing user data

external_apis.name: OpenAI API (or Anthropic API)
external_apis.purpose: Primary LLM inference for conversational coaching (full-tier model) and workout generation (haiku/mini-tier model); prompt caching enabled to reduce repeat-context costs
external_apis.cost: Existing $200/month budget ceiling; no additional acquisition cost — optimize via tiered routing designed by Nova and Algorithm Lab

external_apis.name: OpenAI Embeddings API (text-embedding-3-small)
external_apis.purpose: Vector embedding generation for competitor review corpus and behavioral economics / exercise science RAG document stores; text-embedding-3-small at $0.02/1M tokens is the cost-optimal choice
external_apis.cost: ~$1–$3 one-time for initial corpus embedding at estimated 10M tokens total; negligible ongoing cost for incremental updates

external_apis.name: app-store-scraper (OSS Python library) / AppFollow API
external_apis.purpose: Acquisition of App Store and Google Play review corpora for Fitbod, Future, Freeletics, Caliber, and Noom — 500+ reviews per app for sentiment clustering and persona language extraction
external_apis.cost: Free via OSS scraper (app-store-scraper on PyPI); $0–$50/month if AppFollow API required for volume or rate-limit compliance — recommend OSS path first with manual fallback

external_apis.name: Supabase REST + Realtime API
external_apis.purpose: Existing infrastructure for session event log writes, memory store reads/writes, and real-time cost monitoring dashboard — no new integration required, existing plan covers estimated data volume
external_apis.cost: Covered by existing Supabase plan (free tier supports up to 500MB DB, 2GB bandwidth — adequate for current user scale)

external_apis.name: Semantic Scholar API
external_apis.purpose: Programmatic retrieval of peer-reviewed papers on behavioral economics, habit formation, and exercise science for RAG corpus construction
external_apis.cost: Free tier available for academic research queries
